parameters.content:
### New Task Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Title, Assignee, Duedate). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Normalize Task Payload

### Normalize Task Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming task records to a stable schema

Next: Prepare Approval Packet

### Prepare Approval Packet

Shapes the item: writes `requestId`, `summary`, `requestedBy`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Request Approval via Slack

### Request Approval via Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Hold for Decision · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Hold for Decision

Pauses the execution until the resume webhook is called — this is the approval/HITL gate.

Next: Approved?

### Approved?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.body?.decision || $json.decision }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Execute Approved Action (API) · ②→ Record Decline + Notify Requester

### Execute Approved Action (API)

POST https://api.example.com/v1/tasks — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Join Decision Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Record Decline + Notify Requester

slack — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Join Decision Paths · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Join Decision Paths

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Copy to Google Drive

### Archive Copy to Google Drive

googleDrive — `upload` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Stamp Completion Time · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Archive Copy to Google Drive

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

googleSheets — `read` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

parameters.content:
### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Route expenses with Microsoft Teams and Google Drive at scale

**Pattern:** Approval Workflow · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Route expenses with Microsoft Teams and Google Drive at scale. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual task routing is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Task Form Submission → Normalize Task Payload → Prepare Approval Packet → Request Approval via Slack → Hold for Decision → Approved? → Execute Approved Action (API) → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to approval workflow without manual effort
- Anyone automating task routing in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🙋 Human in the loop

The workflow pauses at the Wait node until a human posts a decision to the resume URL (included in the approval message). Approve → the action executes; anything else → recorded as declined. No timeout is set by default — add one under the Wait node's options if approvals go stale.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your task routing process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
